text: Up to 60% of female athletes experience iron deficiency.
Iron deficiency negatively affects endurance performance by 3%-4% in high-level female athletes.
Oral iron supplementation of 100 mg/day elemental iron for up to 56 days improves endurance performance by 2%-20% in iron-deficient female athletes.
specific_value: 2%-20% improvement with 100 mg/day for up to 56 days

text: Iron-deficient non-anemic female athletes with low serum ferritin may have reduced maximal aerobic capacity, which can improve by 6%-15% with 16-100 mg/day elemental iron for 36-126 days.
specific_value: 6%-15% VO2max improvement with 16-100 mg/day for 36-126 days

text: Serum ferritin below 40 µg/L is the threshold for iron deficiency that may impair sports performance in female athletes.
Parenteral iron administration bi-daily over 8-10 days is an alternative to oral supplementation for improving endurance performance in iron-deficient female athletes.
The effect of iron supplementation on anaerobic power is highly variable (-5% to +9%) in iron-deficient female athletes, unlike the more consistent benefits for endurance performance.
Performance decrements from iron deficiency increase with the severity of the deficiency in female athletes across multiple sports.
population: high-level female athletes across multiple sports
